content: Just as the human language learning definition of reflexion frames it as a deliberate metacognitive step to consolidate new vocabulary and contextual understanding from explicit learning exercises, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this structured, curriculum-aligned framing to avoid ungrounded speculative self-correction. This treats an LLM's post-generation reflection not as a generic self-check, but as a targeted 'vocabulary-style consolidation' of the specific domain rules and solution patterns it was just trained on for a given task.
